items.title: Bijlow doorstaat keuring nu wel en tekent in Serie A
items.summary: Justin Bijlow gaat zijn eerste buitenlandse avontuur aan. De doelman maakt per direct de overstap van Feyenoord naar Genoa.

items.title: Tottenham v Borussia Dortmund: Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates; kick-off 8pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us over on Bluesky Thomas Frank has insisted the Tottenham hierarchy are standing with him in the face of the storm gripping the club. The manager’s job is in the balance, his situation precarious after the home defeat against West Ham on Saturday. The Spurs support were so incensed by the result and the continuation of the team’s terrible Premier League form – they have won twice in their past 13 league matches – that they demanded Frank be “sacked in the morning”. Continue reading...

items.title: Inter v Arsenal, Real Madrid v Monaco, and more: Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-offs ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us on Bluesky | Email Will Barry Glendenning has all the latest from Manchester City’s defensive shambles in Norway. Our man on the Cote d’Azur Luke Entwistle takes a look at what’s going wrong with Monaco. Continue reading...

items.title: Bodø/Glimt v Manchester City: Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates; kick-off 5.45pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Barry Erling Haaland: Having grown up in Bryne, a mere 1,948 kilometres south of Bodo, this represents something of a homecoming for the Manchester City striker. The 25-year-old has scored five goals in the Champions League this season and will be hoping to add to his tally following an extremely fallow period in which he has scored just one goal, a penalty against Brighton, in his last seven games in all competitions. Marc Guehi: The England defender completed a £20m move to Manchester City from Crystal Palace yesterday, with Pep Guardiola describing him as a high-class defender who is the “perfect age” and will provide an immediate boost owing to injuries. Sadly, the boost won’t be as immediate as Pep would like because his new recruit is ineligible to play against Bodo/Glimt. Jamie Jackson reports from just north of the Arctic Circle … Continue reading...

items.title: Aston Villa move for Jean-Philippe Mateta after Juventus offer falls short
items.summary: Crystal Palace want £35m and interested in Guessand Ethan Nwaneri is poised to join Marseille on loan Aston Villa have registered interest in signing Jean-Philippe Mateta, with Crystal Palace weighing up a move for Villa’s Evann Guessand as his replacement. Mateta has expressed his desire to leave Selhurst Park this month and has less than 18 months on his contract. The France striker is understood to have agreed terms with Juventus but the Italian side failed to reach an agreement with Palace over a loan that would include an obligation to buy at the end of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: Rosenior wants Enzo to stay amid Madrid links
items.summary: null

items.title: Ronaldo wins long-running Juve legal dispute
items.summary: Juventus have lost an appeal to overturn a court ruling that had awarded their former striker Cristiano Ronaldo millions in owed wages, the Serie A club confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Fulham in talks to sign Man City's Bobb
items.summary: Fulham are in talks with Manchester City over a move for winger Oscar Bobb, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal's Nwaneri set for Marseille loan
items.summary: Marseille are one of several clubs exploring a loan move for Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Howe is onder de indruk van PSV en prijst Botman
items.summary: Manager Eddie Howe van Newcastle United prees PSV in aanloop naar het Champions League-treffen op woensdagavond. De Engelsman was vooral onder de indruk van het spel tegen Liverpool.

items.title: Canadian league set to trial Wenger's offside idea
items.summary: The Canadian Premier League is given permission to trial Arsene Wenger's "daylight" offside rule.

items.title: Botman lacht om rivaliteit met boezemvriend Veerman: 'Geen blad voor de mond'
items.summary: Sven Botman staat woensdag met Newcastle United tegenover PSV in de Champions League. Op de persconferentie in aanloop naar het duel vertelt de Nederlandse verdediger van The Magpies over zijn rivaliteit met goede vriend Joey Veerman, die hij goed kent van zijn tijd bij Oranje. Veerman had, zoals wij hem kennen, geen blad voor zijn mond over de confrontatie.

items.title: VI Live: Bodø/Glimt zet Man City te kijk, NEC slaat razendsnel toe in Groesbeek
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van dinsdag 20 januari. De Champions League gaat na een korte winterstop weer verder! We houden je hier op de hoogte van de ontwikkelingen. De VI Live van Villarreal - Ajax vind je hier .

items.title: Dit kan Go Ahead Eagles verwachten van OGC Nice
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles treft in de voorlaatste wedstrijd van de Europa League-fase het chaotische OGC Nice. Tactische analyse van een ploeg waar in het eerste halfjaar van dit seizoen ongeveer alles mis lijkt te gaan.

items.title: Rosenior adamant PSG and Real Madrid target Fernández’s long-term future is at Chelsea
items.summary: Fernández linked with Champions League winners Chelsea have no intention of selling Argentina midfielder Liam Rosenior has dealt a blow to Paris Saint-Germain’s and Real Madrid’s hopes of signing Enzo Fernández by insisting the midfielder’s long-term future lies at Chelsea. There has been plenty of noise around Fernández over the past week, with reports in France claiming PSG are targeting him as part of their plans to rebuild their midfield this summer. Madrid are also being talked up as a potential destination for the Argentina international, particularly as they are short of a player capable of dictating play, but Rosenior has no doubts about the 25-year-old’s commitment to Chelsea. Continue reading...

items.title: USMNT 2026 World Cup big board 4.0: Pochettino's roster coming into focus
items.summary: We're less than five months away from the USMNT's World Cup opener, and with the clock ticking down, Mauricio Pochettino's squad is coming into focus.

items.title: Refs' boss Webb backs officials over Dalot incident
items.summary: Premier League referees' boss Howard Webb rejects claims that his officials made a mistake by not sending off Diogo Dalot in Saturday's Manchester derby.

items.title: Return of the 0-0 - is the Premier League getting boring?
items.summary: Two goalless fixtures at the weekend mean this season's Premier League has already featured more 0-0 draws than the whole of last term – but does that mean the league has been boring?

items.title: Webb unconvinced Dalot's Doku tackle was red but VAR right not to intervene
items.summary: PGMOL chief Howard Webb is unconvinced that Diogo Dalot’s challenge on Jeremy Doku in Man Utd’s 2-0 win over Man City was worthy of a red card - but is convinced that VAR was right not to intervene.

items.title: Fury set for April comeback fight as 'top three' possible opponent is named
items.summary: Tyson Fury is holding advanced contract talks over a comeback fight in April and Arslanbek Makhmudov is among the 'top three' potential opponent, confirms his manager Spencer Brown.
